title: Polianthes lata
taxonomy: Polianthes lata is classified within the family Asparagaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the asparagus family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other species. Polianthes lata is a genus of flowering plants native to Mexico, and it is a part of the larger Asparagaceae family. The Asparagaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Agavoideae and Nolinoideae subfamilies. Through phylogenetic analysis, Polianthes lata has been placed in the subfamily Nolinoideae, which includes other plants with similar characteristics.

The plant is reported to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. intro: Polianthes lata, commonly known as the Mexican tuberose or butterfly flower, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico, characterized by its showy, fragrant flowers and edible tubers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its use in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Polianthes lata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments, as well as to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of cold and flu.
